Selection Process for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026

There is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Helper posts. Selection is merit-based on academic qualifications. For Supervisor and CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted.

How to Apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 Online

  • Visit the official portal: Go to your state's official WCD / ICDS website or the direct link provided in the vacancy table above. For central information, visit wcd.nic.in.
  • Find the recruitment notification: Click on the "Recruitment" or "Anganwadi Recruitment 2026" section.
  • Download and read the notification: Download and read the official notification PDF carefully before applying.
  • Register / Login: Create a new account using your mobile number and Aadhaar. If already registered, log in with your credentials.
  • Fill the application form: Enter personal details, educational qualification, address, and select your preferred district/ward. Ensure you apply only for a ward where you are a permanent resident.
  • Upload documents: Upload scanned copies of your passport-size photograph, signature, 10th/12th marksheet, Aadhaar card, and any caste/domicile certificate if required.
  • Review and submit: Check all entries carefully before final submission. Download and print the application confirmation / acknowledgement slip for your records.

Documents Required for Anganwadi Application

  • Aadhaar Card (Mandatory)
  • 10th Marksheet & Certificate
  • 12th Marksheet (for AWW)
  • Graduation Certificate (Supervisor)
  • Domicile / Residence Certificate
  • Caste Certificate (SC/ST/OBC)
  • Passport-size Photograph
  • Signature (scanned)
  • EWS Certificate (if applicable)
  • PwD Certificate (if applicable)

About this page: Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 information is compiled by the OnlyJobs editorial team directly from official state WCD / ICDS recruitment notifications. We verify all vacancy details, eligibility, and last dates before publishing. Page is updated daily as new notifications are released. For any discrepancy, always refer to the official notification PDF linked in the apply column above.

Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 – FAQ's

How to apply for Anganwadi Recruitment 2026 online?

Visit your state's official WCD / ICDS portal (link provided in table above). Register with mobile & Aadhaar, fill the application form, upload documents (photo, 10th certificate, Aadhaar, domicile), and submit before the last date. Print the application acknowledgement slip after submission.

What is the eligibility for Anganwadi Worker 2026?

Candidates must have passed 10th or 12th standard from a recognized board. Age limit is 18–35 years (varies by state). Must be a permanent resident of the ward/village for which vacancy exists. Only female candidates are eligible for Worker and Helper posts.

What is the salary of Anganwadi Worker in 2026?

Anganwadi Worker receives a monthly honorarium of ₹10,000–₹20,000 depending on the state (Karnataka and TN are highest at ~₹14,000). Anganwadi Helper gets ₹5,000–₹7,000/month. The Central Government pays ₹4,500/month as base honorarium, and states add their own component on top.

Is there a written exam for Anganwadi jobs?

No, there is no written examination for Anganwadi Worker and Anganwadi Helper posts. Selection is purely merit-based on academic qualification marks (10th / 12th percentage). For Supervisor / CDPO posts, a written exam or interview may be conducted depending on the state.

What is the age limit for Anganwadi Helper?

Age limit is 18 to 35 years as of 1st January 2026 in most states. SC/ST candidates get 5 years relaxation (maximum age 40). OBC candidates get 3 years relaxation (maximum age 38). PwD candidates get additional relaxation as per state rules.

What documents are required for Anganwadi job application?

Required documents: Aadhaar card, 10th/12th marksheet and certificate, domicile/residence certificate, caste certificate (SC/ST/OBC), passport-size photograph, scanned signature, and EWS/PwD certificate if applicable. Graduation certificate is needed for Supervisor post applications.

What is ICDS recruitment 2026?

ICDS stands for Integrated Child Development Services - the government programme under which Anganwadi centres operate nationwide. ICDS recruitment 2026 refers to hiring of Anganwadi Workers, Helpers, Supervisors, and CDPOs by state WCD departments under the Ministry of Women & Child Development (wcd.nic.in).

Is Anganwadi Worker a permanent government job?

Anganwadi Workers and Helpers are honorary workers, not regular government employees. They receive a fixed monthly honorarium (not salary) and are entitled to benefits like ESI, health insurance, and maternity benefits. Supervisors and CDPOs are regular government employees in most states and receive full pay & grade pay.

Can I apply for Anganwadi job from a different district?

No. Anganwadi Workers and Helpers must be permanent residents of the same ward/village/district where the vacancy exists. A domicile or residence certificate is mandatory. Applications from outside the specified locality are rejected. For Supervisor/CDPO posts, the same district residency rule generally applies.

Forest Department Posts List

Forest departments work with a clear hierarchy from senior Indian Forest Service officers to field-level staff like Forest Guard and Forest Watcher. Understanding these posts helps candidates know growth opportunities and career progression in forest services.

  • Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (PCCF) / Head of Forest Force
  • Additional Principal Chief Conservator of Forests (APCCF) / Additional Chief Conservator of Forests
  • Chief Conservator of Forests (CCF)
  • Conservator of Forests (CF)
  • Deputy Conservator of Forests (DCF) / Divisional Forest Officer (DFO)
  • Assistant Conservator of Forests (ACF)
  • Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Range Forest Officer (RFO)
  • Deputy Forest Ranger / Forest Inspector
  • Section Forest Officer / Forester / Van Daroga
  • Beat Forest Officer / Beat Guard / Forester Grade I
  • Forest Guard / Van Rakshak
  • Forest Watcher / Forest Worker / Plantation Worker
  • Forest Ranger (in states where used separately)
  • Field Guard

Most forest department recruitment notifications on this page focus on Forest Guard, Forester, Forest Range Officer, Field Guard and similar Group B, C and D posts.

Eligibility for Forest Department Jobs

Eligibility for forest department jobs depends on the post, state and recruitment authority. Below is a general idea of qualification and age for popular posts like Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer. Candidates should always check the official notification for detailed eligibility criteria.

Education Qualification for Forest Guard, Forester, Ranger

  • Forest Guard / Field Guard / Forest Watcher / Beat Guard: Usually 10th pass or 12th pass from a recognized board, knowledge of local language, and basic physical standards as per state rules.
  • Forester / Section Forest Officer / Deputy Forest Ranger: Generally 12th pass or graduate in any stream or science-related subjects, with physical standards and sometimes driving or technical skills depending on the post.
  • Forest Range Officer (RFO) / Forest Ranger / ACF: Normally graduate degree in Science, Agriculture, Forestry, Environmental Science, Engineering or related disciplines, with higher physical standards and sometimes a competitive exam similar to state PCS level.
  • IFS / Indian Forest Service and senior posts (DCF, CF, CCF, PCCF): Filled through UPSC Indian Forest Service Examination and promotions, requiring a graduate degree and all-India competitive selection.

Age Limit & Relaxation

  • Forest Guard / Forester / Field Guard: Minimum age is usually 18 years and maximum age is around 27–30 years, with age relaxation for SC, ST, OBC, EWS, Ex-servicemen and other categories as per government rules.
  • Forest Range Officer / ACF and higher competitive posts: Age limit often ranges from 21 to 32 years (varies by recruitment), with relaxations for reserved categories.
  • Exact age limit, cut-off date and relaxation details always depend on the particular notification, so candidates should verify from the official advertisement before applying.

Forest Department Jobs Salary Overview

Forest department salary depends on the post, pay level, state and allowances. Senior officers under Indian Forest Service receive higher pay levels, while Forest Guard, Forester and similar posts fall under state government pay matrices.

Forester / Forest Sub-Inspector Level 6 ₹35,400 ₹45,000 – ₹56,000 Group C
Forest Beat Officer / Beat Guard / Junior Ranger Level 4 ₹25,500 ₹33,000 – ₹42,000 Group C
Forest Range Officer (FRO) / Ranger Level 10 ₹56,100 ₹70,000 – ₹90,000 Group B

Selection Process & Exam Pattern

The selection process for Forest Department Jobs 2026 typically includes written examinations, physical tests and document verification. Some posts also conduct medical examination and walking tests due to the demanding nature of field duties.

Written Exam, PET/PST & Document Verification

  • Written Exam: Most Forest Guard, Forester and Forest Range Officer recruitments start with a written exam based on objective-type questions (MCQs).
  • PET / PST (Physical Efficiency Test / Physical Standard Test): Candidates who qualify written exam are called for running, walking test, height, chest measurement and other physical parameters as per post and gender.
  • Document Verification: Shortlisted candidates must produce original certificates of age, qualification, caste, residence, sports/NCC and other documents mentioned in the notification.
  • Medical Examination: Many forest posts require medical fitness to ensure candidates can perform field duties in forests, wildlife areas and difficult terrain.

Syllabus Highlights for Forest Guard & Ranger

Typical syllabus for written exam may include:

  • General Knowledge & Current Affairs (national, state-level, environment and forest-related topics)
  • General Hindi / English or regional language (grammar, comprehension, vocabulary)
  • General Mathematics / Quantitative Aptitude (up to 10th or 12th level)
  • General Science and Environmental Studies (forest, wildlife, biodiversity, conservation, climate, basic biology)
  • Reasoning Ability (logical and analytical reasoning)

Exact subject-wise marks, number of questions, negative marking and exam duration will be given in the respective forest department notification.

How to Apply for Forest Department Jobs 2026

Most Forest Department Jobs 2026 applications are accepted either online through official websites or offline via prescribed application forms. Candidates should carefully read the official notification before filling the form.

Online Application Process

  1. Visit the official website of the recruiting body (State Forest Department, State PSC, Staff Selection Board or other government portal).
  2. Find the latest link for Forest Guard / Forester / Forest Range Officer / other forest posts and open the detailed advertisement.
  3. Complete the registration process with basic details like name, mobile number, email ID and verify using OTP if required.
  4. Fill the online application form with personal, educational, address and category information exactly as per documents.
  5. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and other certificates in the prescribed format and size.
  6. Pay the application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card or UPI as per your convenience.
  7. Finally, submit the form and take a printout or PDF of the submitted application and payment receipt for future reference.

Documents Required & Application Fee

  • Recent passport size photograph and signature (scanned)
  • Class 10th and 12th mark sheets and certificates
  • Degree / Diploma certificates for graduate-level posts
  • Caste / Category certificate (SC, ST, OBC, EWS, etc.)
  • Domicile / Residence certificate if required by the state
  • NCC, Sports, Ex-serviceman and other quota certificates if applicable
  • Valid ID proof (Aadhaar Card, PAN, Voter ID, Driving Licence, Passport, etc.)

Why Choose Forest Department Jobs?

Forest department jobs are popular among candidates who love nature, wildlife and field work. These jobs offer job security, government benefits and a chance to work in forest conservation, wildlife protection, eco-tourism and community development.

  • Stable government job with regular salary, pension/NPS and allowances
  • Opportunity to work in forests, wildlife sanctuaries, national parks and protected areas
  • Contribution to environment protection, afforestation and biodiversity conservation
  • Good promotion avenues from Forest Guard to Forester, Forest Range Officer and higher posts in many states.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

The latest Mahanadi Coalfields Limited (MCL) Recruitment 2026 has been announced for Asst. Foreman Electrician (Trainee) and Technician (Electrician) (Trainee) posts! Published on 16 April 2026 at mahanadicoal.in, this new recruitment drive offers 500 openings for eligible candidates. Applicants within the 18 to 30 years age group and possessing a Diploma in Electrical Engineering / ITI in Electrician Trade can apply online from 07 May 2026 to 28 May 2026. Find all details about the MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician new vacancy 2026, including eligibility, online form, and notification PDF here.

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Notification for Mahanadi Coalfields Limited (MCL) Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee) T&S Gr-C (E&M) and Technician (Electrician) (Trainee) Cat-III (E&M) Recruitment 2026 is published vide Ref. No. MCL/HQ/Recruitment/Open/2026/311 dated 16 April 2026 on the official website mahanadicoal.in. Candidates should thoroughly read the official advertisement to check post-wise vacancies, eligibility criteria, age limit, reservation rules, selection process and detailed instructions for filling the application form.

Document Download Link
MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Official Notification Download Notification PDF

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 - Overview

Particulars Details
Recruitment Name MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026
Organization Mahanadi Coalfields Limited (MCL), A subsidiary of Coal India Limited
Post Names Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C (E&M) | Technician (Electrician) (Trainee), Cat-III (E&M)
Total Vacancies 500 posts (150 + 350)
Pay Scale Asst. Foreman: ₹47,330.25 per month | Technician: ₹1,583.32 per day
Essential Qualification Asst. Foreman: Diploma/Degree in Electrical Engineering (AICTE approved) | Technician: Matriculate + ITI (2 yr) in Electrician Trade (NCVT/SCVT) + LT Permit
Age Limit 18 to 30 years as on 28/05/2026 (General/EWS)
Application Mode Online - mahanadicoal.in
Application Fee General/OBC/EWS: ₹1000 + GST ₹180 = ₹1180 | SC/ST/PwBD/Female/ESM/CIL Employees: Nil
Application Start Date 07-05-2026 (10:00 AM)
Application Last Date 28-05-2026 (11:50 PM)
Cut-off Date 28/05/2026
Official Website https://mahanadicoal.in
Ref. No. MCL/HQ/Recruitment/Open/2026/311, dated 16.04.2026

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Post-wise Vacancy Details

A total of 500 vacancies (tentative) will be filled for Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee) and Technician (Electrician) (Trainee) posts under MCL Recruitment 2026. Check post-wise and category-wise vacancy details in the table below.

Name of Post Total
Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C (E&M) 150
Technician (Electrician) (Trainee), Cat-III (E&M) 350
Total 500

Note: Vacancies are tentative. MCL Management reserves the right to increase, decrease or cancel vacancies as per requirement. Total 06 vacancies are reserved (horizontal) for PwBDs for the post of Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C (E&M) only. No PwBD reservation is applicable for the Technician (Electrician) (Trainee), Cat-III (E&M) post as per Ministry of Social Justice and Empowerment guidelines.

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 PwBD Disability Details

Name of Post Physical Requirement Suitable category of Benchmark Disabilities Horizontal Vacancies
Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C (E&M) S, ST, W, BN, MF, SE, RW, H, C HH; OA, OL, BL, LC, Dw, AAV; SLD; MD involving (a) to (c) above 06

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates

Events Important Dates
Publication of Advertisement 16-04-2026
Starting Date of Online Application & Payment Gateway 07-05-2026 (10:00 AM)
Last Date of Submission of Online Application 28-05-2026 (11:50 PM)
Cut-off Date for Age & Eligibility 28/05/2026
Tentative Date of Admit Card Download Will be notified on website
Tentative Date of Computer Based Test (CBT) Will be notified on website
Tentative Date of Declaration of Results Will be notified on website

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Eligibility Criteria

Sl. No. Name of Post Eligibility / Essential Qualification
1 Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C (E&M) Diploma in Electrical Engineering (Minimum 3 years course) approved by AICTE OR Degree in Electrical & Electronics Engineering approved by AICTE
2 Technician (Electrician) (Trainee), Cat-III (E&M) (i) Matriculate or equivalent examination passed from any recognized Board of examination. (ii) ITI (2 year course) in Electrician Trade along with Trade certificate issued by NCVT/SCVT and minimum 1 year training/on the job practical experience. (iii) LT permit under IE rules for Mines or 440-550 Volts mining parts permit issued by Competent Authority.

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

Age is calculated as on the cut-off date 28/05/2026. Candidate must not be below 18 years of age as on 28/05/2026. The upper age limit is 30 years for General (UR) and EWS category candidates.

Sl. No. Category Age Relaxation
01 UR/EWS No relaxation
02 OBC (Non Creamy Layer) Central List 3 years
03 SC/ST 5 years
04 Ex-Servicemen (ESM) Actual period of service in defence + 3 years
05 Departmental Candidates (all employees of CIL & its subsidiary companies) No upper age limit
06 PwBD candidates (Only for Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C (E&M)) General/EWS: +10 years | OBC-NCL: +13 years | SC/ST: +15 years

Note: The actual upper age limit of the applicant (except Sl. No. 05) on the crucial date 28/05/2026 shall not exceed 56 years. There is no age bar for Departmental Candidates i.e. any employee of CIL and its subsidiary companies having the requisite qualification.

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Application Fee

Category Fee
General (UR) / OBC (Creamy Layer & Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS ₹1,000/- + GST ₹180/- = ₹1,180/- (Non-Refundable)
SC / ST / PwBD / ESM / Female candidates / Employees of Coal India Limited and its Subsidiaries Nil (Exempted)

Fee can be paid online via Debit Card / Credit Card / Net Banking only. No other mode of payment will be accepted. Fee once paid will not be refunded under any circumstances.

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Exam Pattern

The selection methodology will comprise of Computer Based Test (CBT) only. No personal interview will be held. The CBT will consist of objective type questions with multiple choice answers. There will be no negative marking for wrong answers. Instructions will be bilingual (English/Hindi).

The CBT will consist of two sections totalling 100 marks: Section "A" comprising 20 questions on General Awareness/Aptitude (1 mark each), and Section "B" comprising 80 questions on Technical Knowledge (1 mark each). Total time: 2 hours.

Section Subject No. of Questions Marks
Section A General Awareness / Aptitude 20 20
Section B Technical Knowledge 80 80
Total   100 100

Qualifying Marks in CBT:

Category Qualifying Marks
General/EWS Candidates 45 marks (45% of 100)
OBC (NCL) Candidates 40 marks (40% of 100)
SC/ST Candidates 35 marks (35% of 100)

Candidates may opt for three Test Cities from the list as per their preference at the time of applying. The same cannot be changed after the online form is submitted. MCL reserves the right to allocate or change the Test City.

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Selection Process

The selection for MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 will be based on the following stages:

Computer Based Test (CBT): Eligible candidates will be provisionally called for CBT on the basis of their self-declaration regarding eligibility. Scrutiny/verification of eligibility claims will be done post-declaration of CBT results for candidates called for document verification.

Document Verification: Based on the merit order of marks secured in CBT, shortlisted qualified candidates in the ratio of 1:3 will be called for document verification. In case of a tie in marks, it will be resolved on the basis of Date of Birth (elder candidate first) and thereafter Name alphabets. Candidates must appear with all original certificates/documents and photocopies as per eligibility criteria. Candidates working in Govt./Semi Govt./Autonomous Bodies/PSUs/CIL & Subsidiaries must produce a "No Objection Certificate" from their employer.

Provisional Select List: After verification of documents/certificates, a Provisional Select List of successful candidates will be published on the official website of MCL (https://mahanadicoal.in).

Final Appointment: Provisionally selected candidates will be issued an offer of appointment based on their position in the merit list and with due consideration of reservation policy of Govt. of India for SC/ST/OBC(NCL)/PwBD/EWS/ESM. The validity of the merit panel will be for 01 year or as per extant Govt. guidelines.

Medical Fitness: Selected candidates will have to undergo Initial Medical Examination (IME) in the designated Hospital by the Company's Doctor. Final appointment/joining will be subject to being declared medically fit as per rule. Minimum required height of the candidate should be 60 inches (excluding for the post of Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (Trainee), T&S Gr-C only in case of Dwarfism).

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Career Growth

  • For the post of Asst. Foreman (Electrical) (E&M), further career growth of candidates to T&S Gr-B and onwards will be subject to acquisition of Electrical Supervisorship Certificate of Competency valid for Mines as per the Cadre Scheme.
  • For the post of Technician (Electrician) (E&M), trainees in Cat-III will be regularized as Electrician, Cat-IV after successful completion of one year training in E&M cadre and possession of valid minimum requisite Electrician Statutory permit/certificate.

How to Apply for MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026

Eligible candidates must submit their application online through the official website mahanadicoal.in under CAREERS >>>> Recruitment section. No other means/mode of application will be accepted.

Step 1 - Read Instructions: Read Important Instructions carefully and click on (√) "I Agree" button.

Step 2 - Register: Register by filling necessary details (Post opted, name, Mobile No. and email ID) and click on Submit Button. Note the Application Sequence No., User ID and Password received on your email and mobile number.

Step 3 - Login & Fill Application: Re-login using User ID and Password. Click on the Application Form option, fill all necessary details in the respective fields and upload required documents, Photo and Signature.

Step 4 - Preview & Submit: Check Preview of the Application Form and make corrections if any. Press the Submit Button.

Step 5 - Pay Fee & Print: Make online payment through Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking (as applicable). Take a printout of the application form for future record.

Note: The last date for submission of application is 28/May/2026 up to 11:50 PM. Candidates are advised not to wait till the last date and time for applying online. MCL shall not be responsible if candidates are unable to submit applications at the last hour due to network congestion.

MCL Asst. Foreman Electrician Technician Recruitment 2026 Documents Required

Keep the following scanned documents ready (valid as on cut-off date) before filling the online application:

  • E-Mail ID and Mobile Number
  • Personal and Educational Qualification Details
  • Scanned Copy of Matriculation (X) Certificate/Mark sheets/ITI/Diploma/Degree Certificate/Mark sheets (Jpg/Jpeg/PDF format)
  • Scanned Copy of Valid Caste Certificate (OBC (NCL)/SC/ST) and Income Certificates for EWS (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of Ex-Servicemen Certificates (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of PwBD Certificates (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of LT Permit under IE rules for Mines or 440-550 Volts mining parts permit issued by Competent Authority (if applicable)
  • Scanned Copy of Minimum 01 Year Training/On-the-Job Practical Experience Certificate (if applicable)
  • Details of Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking for Fee Payment
  • Scanned Copy of Recent Photograph (not more than 3 weeks old, jpg/jpeg format)
  • Scanned Copy of Signature with Black Ink Pen (jpg/jpeg format)

NHPC Limited, a Navratna Enterprise under the Ministry of Power, Government of India, has invited online applications for 1-year Apprenticeship Training across its various projects and power stations in Graduate, Diploma and ITI trades.

The recruitment offers training slots in disciplines such as Civil, Electrical, Mechanical, Computer Science, HR, Finance, Nursing, Pharmacy and various ITI trades, with a monthly stipend ranging from Rs. 12,000/- to Rs. 15,000/-, along with DBT support under the NATS scheme.

Eligible candidates aged 18 to 30 years can apply online from 15 April 2026 to 06 May 2026, and selection will be based on merit with preference given to Project Affected Families and candidates from the concerned district, state and UT.

NHPC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

NHPC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Important Dates

Event Date & Time
Online Application Starting Date 15 April 2026 (11:00 AM onwards)
Online Application End Date 06 May 2026 (till 5:00 PM)
Interview / Medical Test Date To be notified separately

NHPC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Eligibility Criteria

Educational Qualification

Sl. No. Apprentice Trade Qualification Stipend
1 Graduate (Civil/Electrical/Mechanical/E&C/CS) B.E./B.Tech./B.Sc.(Engg.) in relevant branch ₹15,000/month
2 HR Executive MBA/PGDM (HR/PM&IR/HRM/MSW) ₹15,000/month
3 Finance Executive B.Com ₹15,000/month
4 PR Executive Bachelor Degree with Mass Communication/Journalism ₹15,000/month
5 Rajbhasha Assistant M.A. (Hindi) with English proficiency / M.A. (English) with Hindi proficiency ₹15,000/month
6 Nursing Assistant BSc Nursing ₹15,000/month
7 Physiotherapy Assistant Bachelor of Physiotherapy (B.P.T) ₹15,000/month
8 Supervisor (HR) BBA ₹13,500/month
9 Diploma (Civil/Electrical/Mechanical/E&C/Nursing/GNM/Pharmacy/X Ray) Diploma in respective discipline ₹13,500/month
10 Electrician / Plumber / Fitter / Machinist / Welder / Carpenter / Computer Operator ITI in concerned trade ₹12,000/month

DBT Note: An additional Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT) amount of ₹4,000/month for Diploma Apprentices and ₹4,500/month for Graduate Apprentices is provided by the Government of India under the NATS scheme. The above stipend figures are inclusive of DBT wherever applicable. Candidates must have an active Aadhaar-seeded, DBT-enabled bank account.

Age Limit

Candidates must be between 18 and 30 years of age. Age relaxation for SC/ST/OBC and other reserved categories will be applicable as per Government of India guidelines.

Reservation

Reservation will be followed as per the Apprentice Act 1961. Candidates claiming reservation must submit the requisite certificate in the prescribed Government format issued by the competent authority. Failure to do so will result in the candidate being treated as General category.

NHPC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Selection Process

There will be no written examination or interview. Selection will be entirely based on merit, calculated from the candidates' academic performance.

Graduate Apprentice

  • 10th Class: 20%
  • 12th/Diploma (higher of the two): 20%
  • Engineering/Graduation/PG:60%
  • Total:100

Diploma Technician

  • 10th Class:30%
  • Diploma in Engineering:70%
  • Total:100

ITI Technician

  • 10th Class:30%
  • ITI in Concerned Trade:70%
  • Total: 100

Preference in Selection

Based on merit, preference will be given in the following order:

  1. Candidates from Project Affected Families of the respective Project/Power Station.
  2. Candidates from the concerned district(s) of the State/UT where the project is located.
  3. Candidates from the State/UT where the project is located.
  4. Candidates from other States/UTs based on merit.

NHPC Apprentice Recruitment 2026 - Application Fee

As per the official advertisement, no application fee has been prescribed for candidates applying for any category of NHPC Apprentice posts 2026.

How to Apply for NHPC Apprentice Recruitment 2026

The application process is online only and consists of two steps - first registering on the NAPS/NATS portal, then applying on the NHPC official website.

  1. Register on NAPS/NATS Portal: ITI trade candidates must register on NAPS at apprenticeshipindia.gov.in. Graduate/Diploma trade candidates must register on NATS at nats.education.gov.in. Complete your profile and upload all required documents.
  2. Note your Registration/Enrolment Number: After completing registration, note your NAPS/NATS enrolment number carefully - it will be required for the NHPC application form.
  3. Apply on NHPC Website: Visit nhpcindia.com → Career Section → Engagement of Apprentice → Apply Now. Fill in all required details including trade, personal information, educational qualifications, category and NAPS/NATS enrolment number.
  4. Upload Documents: Upload scanned copies of - NAPS/NATS Registration Form, 10th Marksheet (Age proof), ITI/Diploma/Degree Marksheets, SC/ST/OBC(NCL) Certificate (if applicable), recent passport-size colour photograph, and signature.
  5. Submit & Save: Review all entries carefully and submit the application before 06 May 2026, 5:00 PM. Save/print a copy of the submitted application for future reference.

Note: candidates should apply for only one region/state/trade. 

Important: Applications made through any mode other than the NHPC official website will not be accepted. Candidates awaiting final results of qualifying examinations and those who have already undergone apprenticeship training of 1 year or more are not eligible to apply.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

SECL Recruitment 2026

The South Eastern Coalfields (SECL) Recruitment 2026 notification released for 1,055 posts of Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman on 8 April 2026. Candidates with B.Tech/B.E, Diploma Can Apply Online. The Online application process for SECL Recruitment 2026 will Start on 15-04-2026 and will remain open until 14-05-2026. The candidate shall apply online through SECL website, secl-cil.in.

SECL Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF Download

The SECL Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF has been released on 08-04-2026 at secl-cil.in. Check the complete job details, vacancy, age limit, application fee, selection process and how to apply from the article. You can Check all Latest  Updates of All Central Government Jobs and State Government Jobs.

SECL Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman Recruitment 2026 Notification PDF

Name of the Post: SECL Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman Online Form 2026

Post Date: 09-04-2026

Latest Date: 15-04-2026

Total Vacancy: 1,055 Posts

Brief Information: The South Eastern Coalfields (SECL) has announced a notification for the recruitment of Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman Vacancy. Eligible Candidates who are interested in the vacancy details & completed all eligibility criteria can read the notification & Apply.

SECL Recruitment 2026 Notification Overview

The South Eastern Coalfields (SECL) has officially released a recruitment notification for Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman. For all details regarding the recruitment process, eligibility, and application procedure, refer to the official notification. Eligible candidates can download it from the link below.

South Eastern Coalfields (SECL)

Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman Vacancy 2026

WWW.OnlyJobs

Application Fee

  • For Unreserved (UR) /OBC- (Creamy Layer & Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS: ₹1000/- + GST ₹180/- = ₹1180/-
  • For SC / ST / ESM / PwBD / Departmental / Female candidates: Nil

SECL Recruitment 2026 Important Dates

  • Starting Date for Apply Online: 15-04-2026 (10:00 AM)
  • Last Date for Apply Online: 14-05-2026 (11:59PM)
  • Tentative date of downloading the admit card: Will be intimated through official website
  • Tentative Date of Computer Based Test (CBT): Will be intimated through official website
  • Tentative date of declaration of results: Will be intimated through official website

SECL Recruitment 2026 Age Limit

  • Minimum Age Limit: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age limit: 30 Years
  • Age relaxation is applicable as per rules.

Qualification

  • Mining Sirdar: Matriculation / Diploma in Mining Engg. / Degree in Mining Engg. + Valid Mining Sirdarship Certificate (Un-Restricted) + Valid Gas Testing Certificate + Valid First Aid Certificate.
  • Dy. Surveyor: Matriculation / Degree or Diploma in Mining / Mine Surveying Engg. + Surveyors' Certificate of Competency (Un-Restricted).
  • Assistant Foreman (Electrical): Matriculation + Diploma (Minimum 3 years course) in Electrical Engineering from a recognized Institute.

Remuneration

  • Basic Pay: ₹47,330.25 per month + DA, Attendance Bonus, Special Allowance, HRA, Transport Subsidy etc. as per NCWA provisions.

SECL Mining Sirdar, Dy. Surveyor & Assistant Foreman Recruitment 2026 Vacancy Details

Post Name Total
Mining Sirdar T&S Grade-C 577
Dy. Surveyor T&S Grade-C (Mining) 43
Assistant Foreman (Electrical) Grade C 435

Interested Candidates Can Read the Full Notification Before Apply Online
Important Links
Apply Online Click Here
Notification Click here

Selection Process

Selection will be made through Computer Based Test (CBT) only.

  • The CBT will comprise of objective type questions with multiple choice of answers. The Question Paper and instructions will be Bilingual (English/Hindi). However, in case of any error in Hindi version, the English version of the question shall be valid & final.
  • The CBT will consist of two sections (Section – ‘A’ & Section – ‘B’):
    • Section – ‘A’: Questions related to Technical Knowledge (Discipline related) with 80 Multiple Choice Questions (MCQ).
    • Section – ‘B’: Questions related to General Awareness/Aptitude and Knowledge of CIL/SECL with 20 multiple choice questions (MCQ).
  • Each question will carry 1 mark and there is no penalty for wrong answer. No marks will be awarded for un-attempted questions.
  • Syllabus for the Computer Based Test (CBT) is attached as Annexure-A in the notification.
  • Selection of candidates in merit panel will be subject to securing of cut-off marks (Minimum Qualifying marks) in the Computer Based Test for concerned post with respect to their category as mentioned below and fulfilling prescribed eligibility criteria:
Category Qualifying marks in CBT
For General/EWS Candidates 45 marks
For OBC(NCL)/ESM Candidates 40 marks
For SC/ST Candidates 35 marks
  • Result of the Computer Based Test (CBT) will be published on SECL website as early as possible after successful completion of the CBT.
  • Provisional merit panels of candidates who secure the requisite cut-off marks and above and fulfilling the eligibility criteria will be published, in the order of marks scored, after resolving tie.
  • In case of tie of marks between 02 or more candidates, the merit-list will be prepared as per the following criteria: a) Date of Birth (As per the descending order of age of candidates) b) Seniority as per the Date of issue of Statutory Certificate c) In case still tied up, alphabetical order of the name of the candidates as recorded in the application form may be taken into consideration. d) In case still tied-up, the Selection Committee will decide the criteria for deciding the merit position of tied candidates.
  • Final merit panel will be prepared based on the marks obtained in the Computer Based Test.
  • Such candidates who would score equal or above the cut off (Minimum qualifying marks as provided in Table D) and as per vacancy available in each category, would only be notified for appearing for scrutiny/ verification of their testimonials supporting their claim of candidature, in order of their respective merit position drawn on the basis of marks scored in Computer Based Test (CBT) for different positions up to the extent the positions remain unfilled. The candidates shall have to appear along with all original certificates/ documents as per eligibility criteria notified in this Employment Notification for scrutiny/ verification. The date, time and venue for scrutiny/ verification shall be notified on SECL website and the candidates shall have to report accordingly. In the event of failure to report with all original certificates and testimonials within the stipulated time, the candidature of such candidate should stand cancelled.
  • Provisional Appointment Letter will be issued to the successful candidates in accordance with their relative merit position and with due consideration of the reservation of posts to the reserved category candidates and against vacancies.
  • If it is found during the Scrutiny/ Verification, or during the course of training, or even during the course of employment, that the candidate is not fulfilling the eligibility criteria in any respect, suppressing of facts or false declaration pertaining to his Employment/Caste/ Testimonials etc. then his/her candidature/ employment will be cancelled/terminated.

Note: In case, a candidate, selected for a particular post & declared as UNFIT during Initial Medical Examination, the case would be referred to Apex Medical Board of the Company within one month based on the written/physical representation and the decision of the Apex Medical Board will be final. If the Candidate is declared UNFIT by the Apex Medical Board, his/ her selection shall stand automatically cancelled. Such candidates will be communicated the decision of the Apex Medical Board as well as regarding cancellation of their selection.

2. How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through SECL website. No other mode (post, email, offline) will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website of SECL https://secl-cil.in/ → Career → Employment Notification for Direct Recruitment of various Statutory posts → Apply online.
  2. Employment Notification is available in both English and Hindi. Candidates may select either of them at their convenience.
  3. While applying online, candidate needs to upload the scanned copies of:
    • Recent passport size colour photograph (not older than 3 weeks in jpg/jpeg format)
    • Signature with Black ink pen (in jpg/jpeg format)
    • Matriculation certificate or equivalent examination certificate or Degree or Diploma Certificate for the post of Mining Sirdar & Dy. Surveyor (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Matriculate or equivalent examination passed from any recognized Board of examination & Diploma or relevant and higher qualification (Minimum 3 years course) in Electrical Engineering from any Institute recognized by the Government, for the post of Assistant Foreman (Electrical) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Valid Caste Certificate OBC (NCL)/SC/ST candidates and Income Certificate for EWS Candidates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Ex-Servicemen Certificates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • PwBD Certificates (if applicable) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Competency certificate of Mining Sirdar/Surveyor (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
    • Gas Testing & First Aid Certificate (for Mining Sirdar only) (in jpg/jpeg/PDF format)
  4. Register by filling up necessary details (Post opted, name, Mobile No. and e-mail ID and click on Submit Button).
  5. Check the User ID and password received on your e-mail and mobile number after successful registration.
  6. Re-login to your account entering the User ID and Password received on Mobile No. and e-mail ID.
  7. Click on the option to go to application form and fill all the necessary details in the respective fields and upload all the required documents including Photograph and Signature.
  8. Check Preview of the Application Form and make corrections, if any.
  9. Press Submit Button and make Online Payment of the fees through Debit Card/Credit Card/Net Banking (as applicable).
  10. Keep the print out of the online application form filled for future references.

Application Fee:

Category Amount (₹)
Unreserved (UR) /OBC- (Creamy Layer & Non-Creamy Layer) / EWS ₹1000/- + GST ₹180/- = ₹1180/-
SC/ ST/ESM/PwBD/ Departmental Candidates/Female Candidates Nil

Note: Applications without the prescribed fee will not be entertained and summarily rejected. No representation against such rejection would be entertained. Fee once paid shall neither be refunded nor be adjusted under any circumstances. Any tax or Bank charges other than the application fees will be borne by the candidates. Application fee will be paid through online mode only.

General Instructions

  • Application should be submitted through ONLINE Mode only.
  • All qualifications should be recognized by AICTE / UGC / appropriate Indian Statutory Authorities.
  • Before submitting the application, the candidate must ensure that he/ she fulfills all the eligibility criteria and other norms mentioned in the Employment Notification. He/ she may cross check the information such as Date of Birth, Category, Contact Details, and Qualifications etc. furnished in the application form before finally submitting the same online as no correction would be possible later and no correspondence will be considered in this regard.
  • The decision of SECL management regarding eligibility criteria, acceptance or rejection of applications, penalty for false information, mode of selection, selection and posting of selected candidates, will be final and binding on the candidates and no enquiry/correspondence will be entertained in this regard.
  • Mere issuance of Admit Card/ Call Letter to a candidate will not imply that his/ her candidature has been finally cleared by SECL. The candidature shall remain provisional till such time SECL verifies the eligibility conditions with reference to original documents after the candidate qualifies in the CBT and confirms appointment.
  • Candidates should ensure that they fulfill the eligibility criteria prescribed for the post for which they have applied. In case it is found at any stage of the selection process or even after the appointment that the candidate has submitted false or incorrect information or suppressed any relevant information/ material facts or does not fulfill the eligibility criteria, his/her candidature/ service is liable for rejection/ termination without notice.
  • No relaxation in the minimum educational qualification, as prescribed in Clause - '2' of this Employment Notification, is admissible for any category of candidate.
  • Wherever Grade (CGPA/ OGP/ DGPA etc.) is awarded, a certificate duly issued by concerned Board/University for conversion of Grade to percentage is to be produced by the candidate at the time of Scrutiny/ Verification of Original documents.
  • Age and all other eligibility criteria shall be reckoned as on the cut-off date as prescribed under this Employment Notification.
  • Candidates should carefully fill up their category (General/ SC/ ST/ OBC- Non Creamy Layer/ Ex-Servicemen/ EWS/PwBD etc.) in the Online Application Form. No change of category shall be allowed at the later stage of selection process.
  • Ex-servicemen must produce discharge certificate issued by Army/Navy/ Air Force of Indian Union at the time of document verification.
  • All documents (Caste Certificate, Educational Qualifications, EWS certificate, etc.) as per applicability and as provided by the candidate will be verified from the office of issuing authority and candidature will be cancelled if the certificates are not found satisfactory/ genuine at any subsequent stage of Computer Based Test (CBT).
  • The candidates must have an active E-mail Id & Mobile number which must remain valid for at least next one year. All correspondences with the candidates shall be done either through Speed Post/ Registered e-mail id & Mobile number (as may be necessary) and all general information shall be provided through SECL website. However, admit card will be provided only through SECL website and the candidates will have to download the admit card. The responsibilities of receiving, downloading and printing of application form, admit card and other information shall be of candidates. SECL will not be responsible for any loss of document due to invalid/ wrong/ incomplete contact details provided by the candidate or for delay/ non-receipt of information. In case of any problems faced by the candidates in filling up the online application they may refer the "Help Desk Option" available in the individual login portal. For queries other than the Online Application Form, candidates may write to Email Id: [email protected] only or can call us on 9425598240.
  • Candidates are advised to apply much before the closing date for submission of Online Application mentioned in this Employment Notification to avoid last minute network congestion.
  • SECL will not take any responsibility for the candidates not being able to submit their applications Online within the prescribed closing dates on account of any reasons beyond the control of SECL.
  • SECL reserves the right to shortlist candidates for Computer Based Test, reschedule the test date & venue etc. or alter any of the advertised condition depending upon the circumstances.
  • SECL will not bear any liability on account of service bond/ salary/ leave salary/ pension contribution, etc, if any, of previous employment of any candidate already working in Central Govt./ State Govt./ Autonomous Body/ Public Sector Undertaking.
  • Candidates employed in Govt./ Semi Govt./ Public Sector Undertaking (including departmental candidates) / Autonomous Bodies are required to produce 'No Objection Certificate' at the time of Scrutiny /Verification of original documents if shortlisted.
  • Selected candidates could be posted in any of the Mines/Establishments of SECL and are liable to be transferred to other subsidiaries of Coal India Limited any time during their service as may be required.
  • Appointment of selected candidates will be subject to being found medically fit in the Company's Medical Examination as per the laid down Rules related to Medical Examination of CIL.
  • Selection of candidate shall be provisional, subject to verification of documents relating to eligibility criteria, character, antecedents and other documents submitted by the candidate and is also subject to his/ her meeting other requirements applicable for appointment under the rules of SECL.
  • Appointment of the candidate in SECL will be subject to verification of Character & Antecedents by the prescribed authorities and if any discrepancy / suppression is found, the candidature of the candidate will be cancelled and decision of SECL in this regard shall be final.
  • The date of Computer Based Test (CBT) and other details will be subsequently informed. Candidates will have to appear at the Centre/ Venue allotted to them by SECL for the Computer Based Test (CBT). No change in Centre/ Venue is permissible and no application shall be entertained in this regard.
  • SC/ ST candidates called for Computer Based Test (CBT) will be paid up to IInd Class Non-AC fare as per rules. However they have to submit their claims in duly filled appropriate TA Claim format provided at the examination centre along with all desired enclosures like caste certificate, photocopy of Onward (mandatory) and return Journey (optional) of railway ticket / bus receipt ticket to the room invigilator after attempting Computer Based Test (CBT) only, subsequent claims as well as claims with incomplete information will not be entertained. The distance of examination centre will be calculated from the Permanent address of the candidate or from place of actual journey whichever is shorter. In case, candidate has submitted only onward journey ticket and not submitted return journey tickets then the claims will be considered up to the double of onward journey fare and that will be paid as per rule.
  • No TA/DA will be paid for appearing in the CBT, scrutiny/ verification of certificates and documents or for joining on being selected except otherwise as provided in this Employment Notification.
  • Candidate will appear for the CBT at the exam center at his/her owns risk and South Eastern Coalfields Limited will not be responsible for any injury or losses etc.
  • SECL management reserves the right to modify/ alter/ restrict/ enlarge/ cancel the recruitment process, and revise the number of vacancies, at its discretion without assigning any reasons whatsoever. The decision of the SECL management will be final and no appeal will be entertained in this regard.
  • All future communication/ information regarding this recruitment will be made available on SECL website. Candidates are advised to check/ visit SECL website regularly for further updates.
  • Court of Jurisdiction for any dispute will be Honorable High Court of Chhattisgarh, Bilaspur.
  • In case of any ambiguity/ confusion, English Version of Employment Notification will be considered valid.
  • Canvassing in any form will be treated as dis-qualification.
  • At the time of Computer Based Test (CBT), if a candidate is found guilty of using unfair means or impersonating or misbehaving in the examination hall or resorting to any other unfair means in connection with his/her candidature for the selection; or obtaining support of his/ her candidature by any means, such candidates will be liable to criminal prosecution and will be disqualified from the Computer Based Test (CBT). The Company may also debar such candidates from participating in any selection process in the Company in future.
  • If it is detected that any candidate has furnished any false information / particulars with regard to his/ her qualification, Category/caste and experience in the Application Form or in any document and/ or misrepresented / suppressed any material fact in the application form, for securing appointment, his/ her service will be terminated forthwith during probation period or even after confirmation of the service.
  • The Candidates whose postings are finalized after CBT, Verification and IME will be disqualified if they put external pressure for place of posting.

Punjab and Sind Bank SO Vacancy 2026 Notification OUT

A recent official notification has been released by Punjab & Sind Bank for the recruitment of Specialist Officers (Security) in MMGS-II on a regular basis. As per the notification, a total of 27 Security Manager vacancies have been announced across various categories, offering a valuable opportunity for candidates with defense or police background to secure a stable banking career.

The online application process will begin from 15 April 2026 and will continue until 05 May 2026, giving eligible candidates a limited window to apply through the official website. This recruitment is particularly important as such lateral entry opportunities in public sector banks are not released frequently. Candidates are advised to carefully review the eligibility criteria, including experience requirements as a Commissioned Officer or Police/Paramilitary Officer, before applying.

Punjab & Sind Bank, a Government of India undertaking, has clearly stated that the number of vacancies is provisional and may change based on requirements. The detailed selection process, including written test, screening, and interview, will be conducted as per bank norms, and further updates will be published only on the official website. Candidates should stay alert for updates and ensure timely application submission to avoid last-minute issues.

Selection Process

Selection will be made through shortlisting of applications followed by Interview (or Online Examination + Interview, if conducted by the Bank).

  • Candidates fulfilling the eligibility criteria will be shortlisted for Interview (or Online Test + Interview) based on the information furnished in the online application and supporting documents.
  • The Bank reserves the right to conduct Online Examination / Group Discussion / Interview or any combination thereof as per its requirement.
  • Final selection will be based on the candidate’s performance in the selection process, subject to verification of documents and medical fitness.
  • The Bank may shortlist candidates in the ratio decided by the Bank and call them for Interview.
  • Merit list will be prepared post-Interview and provisional offer of appointment will be issued to the selected candidates.
  • The decision of the Bank in all matters relating to selection shall be final and binding.

How to Apply

Application Mode: Online only through the official website of Indian Bank. No other mode of application will be accepted.

Steps to Apply:

  1. Visit the official website of Indian Bank and go to the “Recruitment” / “Careers” section.
  2. Click on the link for “Recruitment of Specialist Officers - 2026” and register using a valid email ID and mobile number.
  3. Fill the online application form carefully with all mandatory details (personal information, educational qualification, experience, category etc.).
  4. Upload scanned copies of photograph, signature and all required documents/certificates (educational, experience, category, PwBD etc.) as per specifications.
  5. Pay the requisite application fee / intimation charges online.
  6. Submit the application and take a printout of the final submitted application form for future reference.
  7. A candidate can apply for only one post. In case of multiple applications, only the latest valid (completed) application will be retained.

General Instructions

  • Cut-off date for determining eligibility (age, qualification, experience, etc.) is 01-04-2026.
  • Only one application should be submitted by any candidate. Multiple applications will lead to cancellation of candidature.
  • Candidates must ensure that they fulfil all the eligibility criteria mentioned in the advertisement. Mere submission of application does not guarantee eligibility.
  • The number of vacancies is provisional and may vary according to the actual requirement of the Bank.
  • Reservation for SC/ST/OBC/EWS/PwBD will be provided as per Government of India guidelines. PwBD reservation is on horizontal basis.
  • Candidates belonging to reserved categories must upload valid category certificates issued on or before the last date of registration.
  • All educational qualifications and experience must be from Government recognized Universities / Institutions and should be active as on the date of application.
  • The Bank reserves the right to cancel / modify the recruitment process at any stage without assigning any reason.
  • Canvassing in any form will lead to disqualification of the candidature.
  • Decision of the Bank in all matters relating to eligibility, acceptance or rejection of applications, mode of selection, etc. shall be final and binding on the candidates.
  • All future communications (call letters, results, etc.) will be sent only through the registered email ID and SMS. Candidates are advised to keep the same email ID and mobile number active till completion of the recruitment process.
  • Any dispute arising out of this recruitment shall be subject to the jurisdiction of Courts in Chennai only

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026: The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has officially released the exam date for the NTPC Undergraduate recruitment under CEN 07/2025. Candidates who have applied for the Undergraduate level posts can check the complete exam schedule below. The CBT-1 examination is scheduled to be held from 7th May to 21st June 2026 (specifically on 7th, 8th, 9th May and 13th, 14th, 16th to 21st June 2026). All eligible candidates are advised to download their admit cards and prepare accordingly.

Latest Update: RRB has announced the exam date for NTPC Undergraduate positions. The CBT-1 examination will be conducted from 7th May to 21st June 2026 at various centers across India. Candidates can download the exam date notification from the official RRB websites.

Quick Link: Download RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date Notification 2026

Key Highlights - RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026

  • Organization: Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)
  • Post Name: NTPC Undergraduate (Various Posts like Junior Clerk cum Typist, Accounts Clerk cum Typist, Trains Clerk, Commercial cum Ticket Clerk)
  • Exam Date: 7th, 8th, 9th May 2026 and 13th, 14th, 16th to 21st June 2026 (CBT-1)
  • Exam Mode: Online Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Official Website: www.rrbcdg.gov.in (and respective RRB regional websites)
  • Admit Card Release: 4 days before the respective exam date (City Intimation Slip 10 days before)

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026 Complete Details

The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has published the official exam schedule for the NTPC Undergraduate recruitment 2026 under CEN 07/2025. This notification is crucial for thousands of candidates who are preparing for these government positions in Indian Railways. The examination will test candidates' knowledge in General Awareness, Mathematics, and General Intelligence & Reasoning, and only shortlisted candidates will proceed to CBT-2, Typing Skill Test (if applicable), Document Verification, and Medical Examination.

How to Download RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026 Notification?

Candidates can follow these simple steps to check and download the RRB NTPC Undergraduate exam date notification from the official website:

Step 2: On the homepage, look for the "What's New" or "Notifications" section

Step 3: Find and click on the link titled "RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Date 2026" or CEN 07/2025 notice

Step 4: The notification PDF will open in a new window

Link Description URL
Download Exam Date Notification Click Here

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam Pattern 2026

Understanding the exam pattern is crucial for effective preparation. Here's what candidates need to know about the RRB NTPC Undergraduate examination:

  • Exam Mode: Online Computer Based Test (CBT)
  • Total Marks: 100
  • Number of Questions: 100
  • Duration: 90 Minutes
  • Negative Marking: Yes (1/3rd mark deducted for each wrong answer)
  • Subjects/Sections: General Awareness (40 Qs/40 Marks), Mathematics (30 Qs/30 Marks), General Intelligence and Reasoning (30 Qs/30 Marks)

Preparation Tips for RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam

With the exam date announced, candidates should focus on smart preparation strategies:

1. Create a Study Schedule: Divide your time wisely among all subjects based on your strengths and weaknesses.

2. Focus on Previous Year Papers: Solve at least 5-10 previous year question papers to understand the pattern and difficulty level.

3. Take Mock Tests: Regular mock tests will help you manage time and identify areas needing improvement.

4. Revise Regularly: Make short notes and revise them daily, especially for GK and current affairs.

5. Stay Updated: Keep yourself updated with current affairs, especially related to Indian Railways and general awareness.

RRB NTPC Undergraduate Admit Card 2026 - When to Download?

The RRB will release the admit card for NTPC Undergraduate examination approximately 4 days before the exam date. Candidates can download their hall tickets from the official website using their registration number and password. City Intimation Slip will be available 10 days before the exam.

Documents Required on Exam Day:

  • Printed copy of Admit Card
  • Valid Photo ID Proof (Aadhar Card/PAN Card/Driving License/Voter ID)
  • Recent passport-size photographs
  • Required stationery items (as per instructions)
  • Original Aadhaar or e-Aadhaar (biometric verification mandatory)

What After the RRB NTPC Undergraduate Exam?

After successfully appearing in the RRB NTPC Undergraduate examination, candidates should:

1. Result Declaration: The result is typically declared after the completion of all stages. Results will be available on the official website.

2. Answer Key: Preliminary answer keys may be released after the exam. Candidates can raise objections if any discrepancy is found.

3. Next Stage: Qualified candidates will be called for CBT-2, Typing Skill Test (for applicable posts), Document Verification, and Medical Examination.

Disclaimer: The information provided above is based on the official notification released by Railway Recruitment Board. Candidates are advised to visit the official website www.rrbcdg.gov.in for the most accurate and updated information.

📄 Complete Details & Notification

RRB Group D Eligibility Criteria 2026 state that candidates must be Indian nationals (or as per specified rules) who have passed Class 10/ITI/NAC, are within the prescribed 18–33 years age limit (with relaxations for reserved categories), and meet the required medical and physical standards as detailed in the official notification.

The RRB Group D Notification 2026 (CEN 09/2025) defines these criteria for Level-1 posts such as Track Maintainer, Pointsman, Assistant Pointsman, Assistant Loco Shed, and others, with a total of around 22,195 vacancies (revised figures noted in some sources). The recruitment process includes a Computer-Based Test (CBT), Physical Efficiency Test (PET), Document Verification, and Medical Examination.

RRB Group D Eligibility Criteria 2026 – Quick Overview

Here are the key points at a glance:

  • Minimum Education: 10th pass (Matriculation) from a recognized board, or ITI from NCVT/SCVT, or National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) in relevant trade.

  • Age Range: Generally 18–33 years (calculated as on the cutoff date mentioned in the notification, such as 01.01.2026).

  • Nationality Requirement: Primarily Indian citizens; certain other categories like subjects of Nepal/Bhutan or specified migrants are eligible with proper certificates.

  • Physical Efficiency Test (PET) and Medical Standards: Mandatory qualifying stages after CBT.

  • Category-wise Age Relaxation: Available for OBC, SC/ST, Ex-servicemen, PwBD, and others as per rules.

Parameter

Details (as per 2026 notification)

Exam Name

RRB Group D Level 1 Posts 2026

Conducting Body

Railway Recruitment Board (RRB)

Education Required

10th pass / ITI / NAC from recognized institution

Age Limit (General)

18–33 years (as on cutoff date, e.g., 01.01.2026)

Nationality

Indian citizen or as per RRB rules

Physical Test

PET + medical fitness mandatory

Official Notification

CEN 09/2025 (RRB Group D Notification 2026)

Check the RRB Group D Notification 2026 PDF and Apply Online link below before applying (links available on official RRB websites like rrbapply.gov.in or regional portals during active periods).

RRB Group D Educational Qualification 2026

The minimum educational qualification for RRB Group D 2026 requires candidates to have:

  • Passed Class 10/Matriculation from a recognized board, or

  • ITI certificate from NCVT/SCVT in a relevant trade, or

  • National Apprenticeship Certificate (NAC) granted by NCVT.

Equivalence like NIOS/open schooling is generally accepted if recognized and mentioned in the official notification—always verify the exact clause in the CEN PDF. Final-year or appearing candidates are typically not eligible; the qualification must be completed by the application closing date.

Common doubts:

  • Gap years or backlogs do not disqualify if the certificate is obtained.

  • Subject stream usually does not matter for non-technical posts.

  • Always cross-check with the official notification to avoid issues.

RRB Group D Age Limit 2026

The general age limit for RRB Group D 2026 is 18 to 33 years, calculated as on the date specified in the notification (e.g., 01.01.2026). Candidates must fall within this range before applying.

Category

Minimum Age

Maximum Age (Base)

UR / General

18 years

33 years

OBC (NCL)

18 years

33 years + relaxation

SC / ST

18 years

33 years + relaxation

RRB Group D Age Relaxation 2026 (Category-wise)

RRB Group D age relaxation 2026 is provided over the upper age limit as follows (exact details in official CEN):

Category

Age Relaxation over Upper Age Limit

OBC (Non-Creamy Layer)

3 years

SC / ST

5 years

Ex-servicemen

As per length of service (generally 3–8 years or more)

PwBD (Persons with Benchmark Disabilities)

10 years (additional for OBC/SC/ST)

Railway staff / Quasi-admin staff

As per norms and service length

Age relaxation is not automatic valid category certificates are mandatory at document verification. Confirm exact figures in the official CEN for 2026.

RRB Group D Nationality Criteria 2026

Under RRB Group D nationality criteria, candidates must be:

  • A citizen of India, or

  • A subject of Nepal or Bhutan, or

  • A Tibetan refugee who came to India before January 1, 1962 (with intent to settle permanently), or

  • A person of Indian origin migrated from specified countries (Pakistan, Burma, etc.) with an eligibility certificate from the Government of India.

Most applicants qualify as Indian citizens.

RRB Group D Physical Eligibility & PET 2026

The RRB Group D physical standards include two stages: PET (qualifying) and medical examination.

Physical Efficiency Test (PET) Requirements

PET is qualifying (pass/fail) and conducted for shortlisted CBT candidates. Typical requirements (as per notification; may vary slightly):

  • Male candidates: Running a specified distance in given time, lifting/carrying weight over distance.

  • Female candidates: Adjusted distance/weight parameters.

Only those passing PET proceed further.

RRB Group D Medical Standards 2026

The medical exam follows document verification and includes vision (distant/near), color vision, blood pressure, cardiac, hearing, and general fitness checks. Posts fall under A-2, B-1, C-1, etc., categories candidates must meet the standards for allotted posts.

Common disqualifying reasons include poor eyesight beyond limits, uncontrolled chronic conditions, or major disabilities incompatible with duties. Exact conditions are in the official rules.

Important Documents Required for RRB Group D Eligibility Verification

Candidates need these for various stages (especially document verification):

  • 10th/Matriculation certificate and mark sheet.

  • ITI/NAC certificate (if applicable).

  • Category certificates (SC/ST/OBC-NCL/EWS in central format).

  • PwBD certificate (if claiming).

  • Ex-servicemen discharge certificate.

  • Photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, etc.).

  • Passport-size photographs (per specs).

Who Is Not Eligible for RRB Group D 2026?

  • Candidates below 18 or above 33 years (even after relaxation).

  • Those without required 10th/ITI/NAC by cutoff date.

  • Non-eligible nationality or missing certificates.

  • Failing PET or medical exam.

  • Incomplete, incorrect, or forged documents.

  • Debarred by RRB or with disqualifying background (if specified).

Railway Recruitment Boards (RRBs) under the Ministry of Railways have issued the Centralised Employment Notification RRB CEN 01/2026 for the recruitment of 11,127 Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP) posts in Level 2 of the 7th CPC with an initial pay of ₹19,900 across all Indian Railways.

This bumper railway vacancy is a major opportunity for candidates preparing for latest Railway Govt Jobs as well as state-wise railway jobs, because applications for RRB ALP Recruitment 2026 can be submitted online for any participating RRB including Ahmedabad, Chennai, Kolkata, Mumbai, Secunderabad and others.

Eligible candidates aged 18 to 30 years as on 01 July 2026 can apply for RRB Assistant Loco Pilot posts through online mode only from 15 May 2026 to 14 June 2026 (23:59 hrs) by visiting the official recruitment portal rrbapply.gov.in and the respective RRB websites mentioned in CEN 01/2026.

Reading the official document is important to ensure that you meet all requirements before submitting the application form.

Eligibility Criteria

  • Qualifications: As per the detailed CEN No. 01/2026. Post-wise Education Qualification and Medical Standards will be published exclusively on the official websites of the participating RRBs.
  • Age Limit: 18 to 30 years as on 01.07.2026. Age relaxation as per Railway rules will be applicable as mentioned in the detailed CEN.
  • Candidates are advised to verify their primary details using Aadhaar during filing of the online application to avoid inconvenience and delays due to special detailed scrutiny at every stage of recruitment process for Non-Aadhaar verified applications.
  • Name and Date of Birth in Aadhaar shall be updated to match 100% with the Full Name and Date of Birth available in the 10th class pass certificate. Aadhaar also needs to be updated with the latest photo and latest biometrics (Fingerprint & Iris) before filling the online application.
  • All terms, conditions, eligibility criteria, procedures and other requirements governing the recruitment shall be strictly as per the detailed CEN No. 01/2026 (along with any corrigendum) to be published on the official websites of the RRBs.

Salary / Stipend

  • Post: Assistant Loco Pilot (ALP)
  • Pay Level: Level-2 as per 7th CPC Pay Matrix
  • Initial Pay: ₹19,900/- per month
  • Other allowances and benefits as applicable to Railway employees under 7th CPC will be as per detailed CEN No. 01/2026.

Age Limit (as on 01-07-2026)

  • Minimum Age: 18 Years
  • Maximum Age: 30 Years
  • Age is reckoned as on 01.07.2026.
  • Age relaxation for SC/ST, OBC, Ex-Servicemen, PwBD and other eligible categories will be as per Railway rules mentioned in the detailed CEN No. 01/2026.

Application Fee

  • Application fee details are not mentioned in this Indicative (Short) Notification. Fee details will be published in the detailed CEN No. 01/2026 on the official websites of the participating RRBs.

Participating RRB websites include: rrbahmedabad.gov.in, rrbajmer.gov.in, rrbbnc.gov.in, rrbbbhopal.gov.in, rrbbbs.gov.in, rrbbilaspur.gov.in, rrbcdg.gov.in, rrbchennai.gov.in, rrbgkp.gov.in, rrbguwahati.gov.in, rrbjammu.nic.in, rrbkolkata.gov.in, rrbmalda.gov.in, rrbmumbai.gov.in, rrbmuzaffarpur.gov.in, rrbpatna.gov.in, rrbpryj.gov.in, rrbranchi.gov.in, rrbsecunderabad.gov.in, rrbsiliguri.gov.in, rrbthiruvananthapuram.gov.in.

RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 – Download PDF, Response Sheet & Objection Link

The Railway Recruitment Board (RRB) has released the Technician Answer Key 2026 . Candidates who appeared for the Technician examination held on 6th, 9th, 10th, and 13th March 2026 can now check and download the provisional answer key from the official website www.rrbcdg.gov.in.

This recruitment drive aims to fill 6238 vacancies. If you find any discrepancy in the answers, you can raise objections before 27th March 2026 (up to 10 am).

RRB Technician Answer Key 2026
www.OnlyJobs

Post Name Technician (Grade 1 Signal and Grade 3)
Total Vacancies 6238
Exam Date 6th, 9th, 10th, and 13th March 2026
Answer Key Status Released
Release Date 19th March 2026
Last Date for Objection 27th March 2026 (up to 10 am)
Official Website www.rrbcdg.gov.in

Direct Link: Download RRB Technician Answer Key 2026

How to Download RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 ?

Follow these simple steps to download your answer key:

  1. Visit the official website: www.rrbcdg.gov.in or respective regional RRB website
  2. Navigate to the "Answer Key" or "Recruitment" section
  3. Click on "RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 " link (CEN 02/2025: Exam Responses & Objection Tracker)
  4. Log in using your Registration Number and Password/Date of Birth
  5. Your answer key with recorded responses will appear on screen
  6. Download and save the PDF for future reference

RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 – Important Links

Download Answer Key 2026 Click Here
Raise Objection Click Here (via Objection Tracker after login)
Download Admit Card Click Here
Official Website www.rrbcdg.gov.in
Download Mobile App
Join Telegram Channel
Join WhatsApp Channel

How to Raise Objection Against RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 ?

If you disagree with any answer in the provisional key, follow these steps:

  1. Log in to your candidate portal on www.rrbcdg.gov.in or regional RRB site
  2. Go to the "Challenge/Objection" section (RRB Technician Objection Tracker)
  3. Select the question(s) you want to challenge
  4. Upload supporting documents or references (if required)
  5. Pay the objection fee (₹50 per question, refundable if accepted)
  6. Submit before the deadline: 27th March 2026 (10 am)

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered.

RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 – Important Dates

Event Date
Exam Conducted 6th, 9th, 10th, and 13th March 2026
Provisional Answer Key Released 19th March 2026
Last Date to Raise Objection 27th March 2026 (10 am)

How to Calculate Your Score Using RRB Technician Answer Key 2026 ?

Use this formula to estimate your score:

Total Score = (Correct Answers × Marks per Question) – (Wrong Answers × Negative Marking)

Marking Scheme for Technician Exam:

  • Correct Answer: +1 mark
  • Wrong Answer: -1/3 mark (negative marking)
  • Unattempted: 0 marks

This is the re-examination under Advertisement No. 51 for recruitment to Assistant Professor posts in various subjects across government degree colleges and universities in Uttar Pradesh.

UPHESC Assistant Professor 18 April Shift 01 GS Unofficial Answer Key 2026

The commission has not yet released the official answer key. The unofficial GS answer key for 18 April Shift 01 is being prepared on the basis of:

  • Question paper memory‑based inputs from candidates, and

  • Detailed paper discussion videos by subject experts and coaching institutes.

You can check the unofficial GS answer key and paper solution for 18 April Shift 01 in the following ways:

  1. Through analysis videos of coaching institutes on YouTube where faculty solve the complete GS paper and provide question‑wise answers.

  2. Through educational websites which upload shift‑wise question papers and indicative answer keys soon after the exam.

This unofficial key is only for reference so that candidates can:

  • Estimate their tentative marks.

  • Analyse their performance topic‑wise.

  • Understand the expected cut‑off trend for their subject.

Candidates are advised to rely on the official answer key for final marks calculation and to raise objections, if any.

How to Download Official UPHESC Assistant Professor Answer Key (When Released)

  • Go to the official website: uphesc.org or upessc.up.gov.in
  • Click on the “Answer Key” or “Recruitment” section
  • Find the link for Assistant Professor Advt. 51 RE Exam Answer Key 2026
  • Login with your credentials (Registration Number & Password)
  • Download the PDF for your subject / GS paper

How to Calculate Your Score Using Unofficial Answer Key

Step-by-Step:

  1. Download/Match the Unofficial Answer Key Watch the full video analysis for UPHESC Assistant Professor 2026 | 18 April Shift 01 GS Answer Key uploaded by trusted educators. Note down the correct option for each question (1 to 100).

  2. Count Your Correct Answers Compare your marked responses (from the OMR sheet or memory) with the unofficial key. Let’s say you got C correct answers out of 100.
  3. Apply the Formula Your Tentative Score = Number of Correct Answers × 2 Example:
    • If you have 75 correct answers → Score = 75 × 2 = 150 marks
    • If you have 82 correct answers → Score = 82 × 2 = 164 marks
  4. No Deduction for Wrong or Unattempted Questions Since there is no negative marking, leave wrong answers and unattempted questions as they are — they simply add 0 marks.
  5. GS Paper Break-up (for Reference)
    • General Knowledge / GS: Usually 30 questions (60 marks)
    • The remaining questions may include subject-related or common sections as per the paper pattern.

Disclaimer: This UPHESC Assistant Professor Answer Key is unofficial and for reference only. Please wait for the official answer key on the UPHESC website at uphesc.org.

RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Answer Key 2026 - (Unofficial)

The unofficial answer key for RSSB Agriculture Supervisor 2026 has been prepared on the basis of the question paper and student responses collected after the exam held on 18 April 2026. This key is meant to help candidates:

  • Cross-check the answers marked in the OMR sheet.

  • Estimate their probable score using the official marking scheme.

  • Analyse difficulty level and understand expected cut-off trends.

Since the official answer key has not yet been released by RSSB, candidates must treat this as a provisional/unofficial key only.

How to Raise Objection Against RSSB Agriculture Supervisor Answer Key 2026 (Unofficial) ?

  • Wait for RSSB to release the official provisional answer key on rssb.rajasthan.gov.in and use the unofficial key only for rough marks estimation.

  • Download the official answer key and your response sheet, compare them carefully, and note down all questions where you find any mistake.

  • Collect strong proof (standard books, government/official sources, etc.) for each disputed question; avoid relying only on coaching notes or guesses.

  • During the objection window, log in on the RSSB portal, select the Agriculture Supervisor 2026 exam, choose the question numbers, enter your proposed correct answers with reasons, upload documents, and pay the objection fee per question.

  • Submit the objections before the last date, then download and keep the objection receipt safely and wait for the final answer key and result.

Important: Objections submitted after the deadline will NOT be considered. Unofficial keys are for reference only.
